forecast.ground_truth: public ladder (pokemon-tcg-ai-battle): monofighting deck-swap (sub 53836290) = 276.5 vs flat-deck v4 (sub 53796612) = 676.2 — FALSIFIED, lost by ~400 Elo (~0.09 head-to-head win-rate equiv). Self-play (vs greedy/deck-on-deck) said BETTER; the field said catastrophically WORSE — the proxy-vs-ground-truth trap.
forecast.label: swap the sample deck for monofighting, piloted by the heuristic
forecast.local_evidence: real-cabt N=60-300: monofighting>sample 0.60 [0.51,0.68] deck-on-deck (greedy both); heuristic 0.76 [0.67,0.83] vs greedy on monofighting vs FLAT 0.46 on sample
forecast.proposition: the monofighting deck piloted by the heuristic beats the heuristic+sample-deck baseline (648.1) on the ladder, by a margin the games can resolve

forecast.ground_truth: public ladder: rebuilt Abomasnow (sub 53858250) = 225.2 vs v4 starter (sub 53796612) = 719.1 — FALSIFIED, ~494 Elo worse. The board fix HELD (bench 4-5 on the ladder) but the deck still went 2-10 → benching was NOT the bottleneck; cutting energy 35->13 / the 34-trainer build was a major regression. The 35-energy starter was NOT broken. SECOND falsified deck bet (after monofighting) — self-play/container testing misled us into both.
forecast.label: rebuild Abomasnow: cut energy 35->13 + graft a draw/search engine, piloted by the heuristic
forecast.local_evidence: container (heuristic held constant, vs the real extracted Lucario decklist): board fix CONFIRMED (avg max bench 1.0->3.5); self-play win-rate indistinguishable (0.525 vs 0.588) but BLIND to the real field (old = 0.588 self-play / 0.33 ladder)
forecast.proposition: the rebuilt consistent Mega Abomasnow ex deck (13 energy / 34 trainer / 13 Pokemon) piloted by the heuristic beats the v4 starter-deck baseline (~712 ladder) by a margin the games can resolve
